--
Expense ratio
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for managing your mutual fund investments.     It is the total percentage of a company's fund assets used for administrative, management, advertising, and other expenses.
Tax
A percentage of your capital gains payable to the government upon exiting your mutual fund investments.     Taxation is categorized as long-term capital gains (LTCG) and short-term capital gains (STCG) depending on your holding period and the type of fund.
Exit load
A fee payable to a mutual fund house for exiting a fund (fully or partially) before the completion of a specified period from the date of investment.
Stamp duty
A form of tax payable for the purchase or sale of an asset or security.
01 Jan 2019

Tax implication
If you redeem within one year, returns are taxed at 20%. If you redeem after one year, returns exceeding Rs 1.25 lakh in a financial year are taxed at 12.5%.

About
ICICI Prudential Nifty Next 50 Index Direct Growth
ICICI Prudential Nifty Next 50 Index Direct Growth is a Equity Mutual Fund Scheme launched by ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund. This scheme was made available to investors on 12 Oct 1993. Ajay Kumar Solanki is the Current Fund Manager of ICICI Prudential Nifty Next 50 Index Direct Growth fund. The fund currently has an Asset Under Management(AUM) of ₹11,18,142 Cr and the Latest NAV as of 02 Jul 2026 is ₹67.23.
The ICICI Prudential Nifty Next 50 Index Direct Growth is rated Very High risk. Minimum SIP Investment is set to ₹100. Minimum Lumpsum Investment is ₹100.
;
Investment Objective
The objective of the fund is to invest in companies whose securities are included in Nifty Next 50 Index and to endeavor to achieve the returns of the above index as closely as possible.
Fund benchmark
NIFTY Next 50 Total Return Index
